questions.q: Read the following extract from the poem and answer the question. (i) In the poem, what is the central idea of the message that the father wants to convey to his son?
questions.answer: The central idea of the message is to be a responsible citizen and to live a life with values.

questions.q: In Father to Son, the poet highlights the importance of responsibility in life. What message does the poet want to convey to his son through this poem?
questions.answer: The poet wants to convey that a person should take responsibility for his actions and be a good citizen. He should live a life with values and not waste his time, talent and resources.

questions.q: Choose the correct option: The poem 'Father to Son' is written in the form of a _______.
Read the following extract from the poem and answer the question. (i) What does the line 'Do not now stand at street corners throwing flowers at crowds' suggest in the poem?
questions.answer: The line suggests that one should not waste their time and energy in frivolous activities like throwing flowers at crowds.

questions.q: In the poem, the poet advises his son not to become a _______.
Read the following extract from the poem and answer the question. (i) What does the poet mean by the line 'Do not flinch from the task'?
questions.answer: The poet means that one should not shy away from their responsibilities and duties.

questions.q: The poem 'Father to Son' is a reflection of the poet's _______.
What is the central theme of the poem 'Father to Son'?
questions.answer: The central theme of the poem is the importance of responsibility and living a life with values.

questions.q: In the poem, the poet advises his son to be a _______.
Read the following extract from the poem and answer the question. (i) What does the line 'Do not waste your time with idle hopes' suggest in the poem?
questions.answer: The line suggests that one should not waste their time with unrealistic hopes and expectations.
